meaning: adj.伊顿公学的 伊顿公学般的
examples: Reports that the prime minister, who eschews formal dress for fear of reviving his privileged Old Etonian image, would wear a plain suit provoked criticism from many quarters.
Simon Mann, who goes on trial in Equatorial Guinea today, is an Old Etonian adventurer who has spent most of his decidedly mixed career in the murky world of special forces and mercenaries.
Sir Nick, a languid old Etonian with a sardonic sense of humour, is a veteran of such occasions.
Tory MP and Old Etonian Jesse Norman told the Times: "Other schools don't have the same commitment".
Sir George Young, an old Etonian known as the "bicycling baronet, " is one of the most experienced members of the coalition government.
